name: Uninstall-Package

options.name: -AllowClobber
options.description: Overrides warning messages about conflicts with existing commands. Overwrites existing commands that have the same name as commands being installed.

options.name: -AllowPrereleaseVersions
options.description: Allows packages marked as prerelease to be uninstalled.

options.name: -AllVersions
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet uninstalls all versions of the package.

options.name: -Destination
options.description: Specifies a string of the path to the input object.

options.name: -ExcludeVersion
options.description: Switch to exclude the version number in the folder path.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Forces the command to run without asking for user confirmation.

options.name: -ForceBootstrap
options.description: Forces PackageManagement to automatically install the package provider for the specified package.

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Accepts pipeline input that specifies the package's SoftwareIdentity object from the `Get-Package` cmdlet. InputObject accepts the SoftwareIdentity object as a `Get-Package` value or a variable that contains the object.

options.name: -InstallUpdate
options.description: Indicates that `Uninstall-Package` uninstalls updates.

options.name: -MaximumVersion
options.description: Specifies the maximum allowed package version that you want to uninstall. If you don't specify this parameter, `Uninstall-Package` uninstalls the package's newest version.

options.name: -MinimumVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum allowed package version that you want to uninstall. If you don't add this parameter, `Uninstall-Package` uninstalls the package's newest version that satisfies any version specified by the MaximumVersion parameter.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies one or more package names. Multiple package names must be separated by commas.

options.name: -NoPathUpdate
options.description: NoPathUpdate only applies to the `Install-Script` cmdlet. NoPathUpdate is a dynamic parameter added by the provider and isn't supported by `Uninstall-Package`.

options.name: -PackageManagementProvider
options.description: Specifies the PackageManagement provider.

name: -ProviderName
-Provider
options.description: Specifies one or more package provider names to search for packages. You can get package provider names by running the `Get-PackageProvider` cmdlet.

options.name: -RequiredVersion
options.description: Specifies the exact allowed version of the package that you want to uninstall. If you don't add this parameter, `Uninstall-Package` uninstalls the package's newest version that satisfies any version specified by the MaximumVersion parameter.

options.name: -Scope
options.description: Specifies the scope for which to uninstall the package. The acceptable values for this parameter are as follows:

options.name: -SkipDependencies
options.description: Skips the uninstallation of software dependencies.

options.name: -SkipPublisherCheck
options.description: Allows you to get a package version that is newer than your installed version. For example, an installed package that is digitally signed by a trusted publisher but a new version isn't digitally signed.

options.name: -Type
options.description: Specifies whether to search for packages with a module, a script, or both. The acceptable values for this parameter are as follows:

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if `Uninstall-Package` cmdlet is run. The cmdlet isn't run.

name: -Debug
-db
options.description: Displays programmer-level detail about the operation done by the command. This parameter works only when the command generates a debugging message.

name: -ErrorAction
-ea
options.description: Determines how the cmdlet responds to a non-terminating error from the command. This parameter works only when the command generates a non-terminating error.

name: -ErrorVariable
-ev
options.description: Error records are automatically stored in the `$Error` automatic variable. When you use the ErrorVariable parameter on a command, PowerShell also stores the error records emitted by the command in the variable specified by the parameter.

name: -InformationAction
-infa
options.description: Within the command or script in which it's used, the InformationAction common parameter overrides the value of the `$InformationPreference` preference variable, which by default is set to SilentlyContinue.

name: -InformationVariable
-iv
options.description: When you use the InformationVariable common parameter, information records are stored in the variable specified by the parameter.

name: -OutBuffer
-ob
options.description: Determines the number of objects to accumulate in a buffer before any objects are sent through the pipeline. If you omit this parameter, objects are sent as they're generated.

name: -OutVariable
-ov
options.description: Stores output objects from the command in the specified variable in addition to sending the output along the pipeline.

name: -PipelineVariable
-pv
options.description: PipelineVariable allows access to the most recent value passed into the next pipeline segment by the command that uses this parameter. Any command in the pipeline can access the value using the named PipelineVariable. The value is assigned to the variable when it's passed into the next pipeline segment.

name: -ProgressAction
-proga
options.description: Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. The Write-Progress cmdlet creates progress bars that show a command's status.

name: -Verbose
-vb
options.description: Displays detailed information about the operation done by the command. This information resembles the information in a trace or in a transaction log. This parameter works only when the command generates a verbose message.

name: -WarningAction
-wa
options.description: Determines how the cmdlet responds to a warning from the command. Continue is the default value. This parameter works only when the command generates a warning message.

name: -WarningVariable
-wv
options.description: Stores warning records about the command in the specified variable.

description: Uninstalls one or more software packages.
